commands.review_summary:
The PR passes `allowBootstrap: true` through outbound channel selection, avoids bootstrapping when a bundled plugin is already resolvable, and adds focused channel-resolution and channel-selection tests.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection gives a high-confidence path: current main omits `allowBootstrap` in channel selection, while the resolver only lazy-loads an external channel adapter when that flag is true; I did not run the full `openclaw agent --local` flow in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: No repair lane is needed because the PR already contains the narrow code change and tests; the remaining action is maintainer validation and merge handling.

target.title: fix(channels): pass allowBootstrap from channel-selection so in-agent message tool resolves channels in --local processes
target.body:
## Problem

The in-agent `openclaw_message` tool intermittently throws "Channel is unavailable: <channel>" against a configured channel that the CLI send-path (`openclaw message send`) can deliver to seconds before and after. Six occurrences observed in 14 days, all during ~04:00-05:00 UTC heartbeat windows that route dispatches through `openclaw agent --local`.

Adjacent to #77254 (closed 2026-05-07), which fixed the cron-announce and final-reply paths but did not touch the in-agent message tool caller. The underlying resolver path is the same; the caller surface is different.

## Change and value

`src/infra/outbound/channel-selection.ts`'s `resolveAvailableKnownChannel` calls `resolveOutboundChannelPlugin({ channel, cfg })` without `allowBootstrap`. In a process where the channel plugin has not yet been eagerly loaded, such as `openclaw agent --local`, which loads only the hook-only plugin set at startup, the resolver returns metadata-only, which the caller treats as "not available" and short-circuits to `undefined`. Callers in turn surface "Channel is unavailable: <channel>".

Pass `allowBootstrap: true` from this caller. In gateway context, where channels are pre-loaded eagerly, this is a no-op fast path. In agent-local context, the existing bootstrap branch runs once to lazy-load the channel adapter, and all subsequent message-tool calls in that process hit the loaded plugin.

## Who is affected, and who is not

Anyone running agents via `openclaw agent --local` (one-shot dispatches, heartbeats, cron-driven workflows) whose dispatch invokes the message tool against a configured outbound channel is affected.

Gateway-routed invocations are not expected to change because their channel plugin is already loaded eagerly. This PR does not add new channel ids, config, plugin loading policy, or public API surface.

## Why now

Six recurrences in 14 days; the recurring pattern is well-documented and the call chain identified.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: `openclaw agent --local` message-tool dispatch resolves a configured channel after this caller opts into channel bootstrap.
- **Real environment tested**: Stock `2026.5.19` install plus patched bundled `dist/channel-selection-*.js`; configured `discord` channel.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Executed `node probe-channel-resolution.mjs` against configured `discord`; toggled `allowBootstrap=false/true`.
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal transcript from Node resolver probe:

```text
$ node probe-channel-resolution.mjs
allowBootstrap=false -> resolved=false
allowBootstrap=true -> resolved=true id=discord has_send_text=true
```

- **Observed result after fix**: Resolved Discord plugin; `message.send.text` present; metadata miss reproduced for `allowBootstrap=false`.
- **What was not tested**: Full `openclaw agent --local` dispatch through real agent run. Gateway-routed delivery.

## Implementation

One production-line behavior change: add `allowBootstrap: true` to the `resolveOutboundChannelPlugin` call inside `resolveAvailableKnownChannel` in `src/infra/outbound/channel-selection.ts`.

One focused regression test now covers this caller and verifies both explicit-channel and fallback-channel resolution forward `allowBootstrap: true`.

## Verification

- Local: `pnpm test src/infra/outbound/channel-selection.test.ts` - 1 file, 19 tests passed
- Local: `git diff --check`
- Remote Crabbox/GCP: `pnpm check:changed; pnpm test src/infra/outbound/channel-selection.test.ts` on `e2-standard-8` Spot in `us-east1-b`, lease `cbx_c5b4a103b603`, exit `0`
- Review swarm: four read-only lanes over correctness, lifecycle/error paths, tests/proof, and security/public surface; no code findings after PR-body proof wording was fixed
- Claude review gate: no actionable findings
- Codex review gate: `codex review --base origin/main`; no discrete correctness issue found
